_readme: Earnings-revision regime tags for the semiconductor complex. 'rising' = forward EPS estimates being revised up at the time; 'rolling' = estimates peaking or being cut. These are a hand-audited historical reconstruction, not a live data feed — audit and edit them as your own view of each period improves. Untagged dates default to 'unknown'. Keep the current period's end date pushed forward as long as the regime holds.

periods.note: 1995 semi/memory boom, estimates rising into Q3 95
DRAM price collapse, estimate cuts through 1996
Partial recovery pre-Asia crisis
Asian financial crisis estimate cuts
Dot-com/telecom capex boom; revisions still rising through H1 2000 even as valuation detached
Telecom bust, the great estimate collapse
Post-war recovery, revisions up
Mid-cycle inventory correction, 2004 chop
Mid-decade recovery leg
GFC estimate collapse
V-recovery, inventory restock, revisions surging
Euro crisis / PC deceleration cuts
Mobile cycle, consolidation-driven margin lift
2015 industrial/China slowdown cuts
Memory supercycle v1, cloud capex, relentless upward revisions
Memory bust + trade war; estimates rolled while trailing earnings were at records
Bottoming, revisions inflecting up pre-COVID
COVID digital pull-forward, shortage pricing power
Consumer/memory rollover + rate shock; the -46% SMH drawdown
AI capex supercycle v1; the 'unchaseable' May 2023 NVDA gap that ran +60%
Mixed: AI revisions up, analog/auto/industrial down; deliberately untagged
Memory supercycle v2: MU $50B guide, HBM sold out through 2027, Hynix 7x oversubscribed. AUDIT THIS TAG FIRST when revisions inflect.
